# airtelcloud_public_ip (Resource)

Manages an Airtel Cloud Public IP. Public IPs are allocated via NAT against a Virtual Machine or Load Balancer private IP and are availability zone specific.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `availability_zone` (String) The availability zone for the public IP (e.g., `S1`, `S2`).
- `object_name` (String) The name for the public IP allocation.
- `vip` (String) The target private IP (VM or Load Balancer IP) to NAT against.

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `allocated_time` (String) The timestamp when the public IP was allocated.
- `az_name` (String) The availability zone name.
- `domain` (String) The domain of the public IP.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier (UUID) of the public IP.
- `public_ip` (String) The allocated public IP address.
- `region` (String) The region of the public IP.
- `status` (String) The current status of the public IP.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
